remember: Students will be able to recall the definition of a differential equation and its order.
Students will be able to identify the type of a differential equation (Linear or Non-linear).
Students will be able to state the general and particular solutions of a differential equation.
understand: Students will be able to understand the concept of the order of a differential equation.
Students will be able to comprehend the distinction between homogeneous and non-homogeneous differential equations.
Students will be able to grasp the significance of the Lagrange's and Bernoulli's methods of solving differential equations.
apply: Students will be able to apply the Euler's method for the numerical solution of a differential equation.
Students will be able to use the method of separation of variables to solve differential equations.
Students will be able to solve differential equations using the substitution method.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the stability of the solution of a differential equation.
Students will be able to use the concept of the integrating factor to solve linear differential equations.
Students will be able to solve differential equations using the method of variation of parameters.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1: To recall that a differential equation is an equation which contains the derivatives of the dependent variable with respect to the independent variable.
To identify the order of a differential equation.
To state the general and particular solutions of a differential equation.
board_exam_objectives: To solve first-order differential equations including the following types: separable, homogeneous, linear, exact and Bernoulli's equation.
To solve second-order linear homogeneous differential equations with constant coefficients.
To apply Euler's method for the numerical solution of a differential equation.
